creator: martin@email.aon.at
text:
[Note: The e-mail address given above is invalid, as I want to avoid
	getting spam. Please reply by adding to this PR, I'll check back
	regularly.]

	I am running the latest xorg ports on a Fujitsu-Siemens S6120 laptop.
	When using the intel video driver, the system will invariably panic
	after a while of usage. When using the i810 driver instead, this
	does not happen.

	Here are two kgdb sessions:

	[0]# pushd /var/crash
	/var/crash ~
	[0]# kgdb /boot/kernel/kernel vmcore.2
	kgdb: kvm_nlist(_stopped_cpus): 
	kgdb: kvm_nlist(_stoppcbs): 
	[GDB will not be able to debug user-mode threads: /usr/lib/libthread_db.so: Undefined symbol "ps_pglobal_lookup"]
	GNU gdb 6.1.1 [FreeBSD]
	Copyright 2004 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
	GDB is free software, covered by the GNU General Public License, and you are
	welcome to change it and/or distribute copies of it under certain conditions.
	Type "show copying" to see the conditions.
	There is absolutely no warranty for GDB.  Type "show warranty" for details.
	This GDB was configured as "i386-marcel-freebsd".
	(no debugging symbols found)...Attempt to extract a component of a value that is not a structure pointer.
	(kgdb) where
	#0  0xc04aafc2 in doadump ()
	#1  0xc06a9d00 in buf.0 ()
	#2  0xe347bb98 in ?? ()
	#3  0xc04ab41d in boot ()
	Previous frame inner to this frame (corrupt stack?)
	(kgdb) #
	[0]# kgdb /boot/kernel/kernel vmcore.3
	kgdb: kvm_nlist(_stopped_cpus): 
	kgdb: kvm_nlist(_stoppcbs): 
	[GDB will not be able to debug user-mode threads: /usr/lib/libthread_db.so: Undefined symbol "ps_pglobal_lookup"]
	GNU gdb 6.1.1 [FreeBSD]
	Copyright 2004 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
	GDB is free software, covered by the GNU General Public License, and you are
	welcome to change it and/or distribute copies of it under certain conditions.
	Type "show copying" to see the conditions.
	There is absolutely no warranty for GDB.  Type "show warranty" for details.
	This GDB was configured as "i386-marcel-freebsd".
	(no debugging symbols found)...Attempt to extract a component of a value that is not a structure pointer.
	(kgdb) where
	#0  0xc04aafc2 in doadump ()
	#1  0xc06a9d00 in buf.0 ()
	#2  0xe347bb98 in ?? ()
	#3  0xc04ab41d in boot ()
	Previous frame inner to this frame (corrupt stack?)
	(kgdb) #
	[0]# 

	Xorg log, note that the server in this case was terminated by
	terminating xdm, but still received a signal 11:
